workflowSchedules.title: Workflow schedules
workflowSchedules.subtitle: Run workflows on a cron with optional dynamic input keys (fixed, step, loop, random).
workflowSchedules.forbidden: This area is only available for owner and colleague accounts.
workflowSchedules.empty: No schedules yet. Create one to run a workflow automatically.
workflowSchedules.selectHint: Select a schedule in the list or create a new one.
workflowSchedules.confirmDelete: Delete this schedule? Payload rules and run logs for this schedule will be removed.
workflowSchedules.cronEveryTotalMinutesHint: Mappable to one 5-field cron: 1–59 min (step minutes), or every 1–23 hours at :00, or 24h as once daily at midnight. Values like 95 minutes cannot be one classic cron line — use Advanced or another mechanism.
workflowSchedules.cronDailyTimesHint: One classic cron can only use the same minute for every listed hour (e.g. 7:15, 12:15). Different minutes for different hours need Advanced cron or multiple schedules.
workflowSchedules.cronIssueEveryMinutes: This interval cannot be expressed as a single standard 5-field cron. Switch to Advanced or use 1–59 minutes, or a whole number of hours (60, 120, …).
workflowSchedules.cronIssueDailyMixed: These wall-clock times use different minutes; one cron line cannot represent them all. Use the same minute for every hour, Advanced cron, or separate schedules.
workflowSchedules.cronIssueDailyEmpty: Enter at least one valid time such as 7:00 or 12:30.
workflowSchedules.cronSaveBlocked: Fix the schedule expression before saving.
workflowSchedules.cronSaveBlockedMixedTimes: Fix daily times (same minute for all hours), switch to Advanced cron, or create multiple schedules.
workflowSchedules.cronSaveBlockedInterval: This interval cannot be expressed as one standard cron. Use Advanced cron or a supported interval (e.g. 1–59 minutes or 60, 120, …).
workflowSchedules.cronEveryMinutesHint: Uses */N * * * * (1–59). For longer gaps, use daily mode or custom cron.
workflowSchedules.cronDailyHoursHint: Example: 7, 12, 17 → 7:00, 12:00 and 17:00 each day (same minute below).
workflowSchedules.cronDailyHoursRequired: Enter at least one hour (0–23) for daily schedule.
workflowSchedules.workflowIdHint: UUID of the workflow to run.
workflowSchedules.workflowSearchPlaceholder: Search by name, code, or id…
workflowSchedules.workflowPickerUnknownId: This workflow id is not in your current list. Save only if the id is still valid on the server.
workflowSchedules.workflowPickerHint: Select a workflow by name; the API stores the workflow id.
workflowSchedules.workflowSetPayloadHint: When off, the engine passes no custom input. When on, keys below map into workflow input.
workflowSchedules.payloadWarnEmpty: Payload mode is on but there are no keys yet — runs may use undefined input until you add keys.
workflowSchedules.name: Name
workflowSchedules.codeGeneratedHint: Schedule code is generated by the server after creation.
workflowSchedules.payloadReadOnlyHint: This schedule is active or paused — input keys are read-only. Disable the schedule to add or edit keys.
workflowSchedules.payloadReadOnlyBlocked: Payload keys can only be edited when the schedule is disabled. Pause or disable the schedule first.
workflowSchedules.payloadStepValidation: For step type, enter an integer string only (e.g. 1, -2, +5). Non-zero. No decimals or scientific notation.
workflowSchedules.payloadListValidation: For loop/random type, enter at least one item separated by comma or semicolon.
workflowSchedules.payloadStepHint: Step: value must be a non-zero integer delta (e.g. 1, -2, 5).
workflowSchedules.payloadListHint: Loop/Random: separate items by comma or semicolon (e.g. A, B, C).
workflowSchedules.payloadFixedHint: Fixed: plain text, number, boolean, null, or JSON string/object/array.
workflowSchedules.payloadFixedJsonWarn: Value looks like JSON but is invalid — it may be treated as plain text until fixed. Saving anyway.
workflowSchedules.payloadFixedJsonInline: This looks like JSON but is invalid — fix syntax or the resolver may treat it as plain text.
workflowSchedules.payloadTypeChangeStepWarning: Changing type to/from step may reset the step cursor when you save.
workflowSchedules.payloadTypeChangeStepInfo: Payload type changed involving step — the server may reset step cursor.
workflowSchedules.consecutiveFailuresBanner: Consecutive failed runs: {n}. After {max} in a row, the server may auto-disable this schedule.
workflowSchedules.confirmActivateNewSession: Activating from disabled starts a new schedule session (loop turn resets to 1 in the new session). Continue?
workflowSchedules.runTurnTooltip: Turn number in the current schedule session (1-based); used for loop ordering.
workflowSchedules.payloadValueHint: fixed: text/JSON; step: integer delta; loop/random: comma or semicolon separated list.
workflowSchedules.runsEmpty: No runs logged yet for this schedule.

websites.subtitle: Manage external domains and their auth tokens for HTTP integrations.
websites.confirmDelete: Delete this website? This action cannot be undone.
websites.domainHint: Domain is normalized to lowercase, without www. and protocol.
websites.headerNameHint: Required when auth type is API key (e.g. x-api-key).
websites.usernameHint: Required when auth type is Basic.
websites.tokenHintCreate: Token will be stored securely; the server never returns it back.
websites.tokenHintUpdate: Leave blank to keep current token. Enter a new value only if you want to rotate.
websites.empty: No websites yet. Click "Add website" to create the first one.
websites.validationHeaderRequired: Please enter a header name for API key auth.
websites.validationUsernameRequired: Please enter a username for Basic auth.

memory.subtitle: Edit workspace MEMORY.md and manage memory RAG records referenced as @code in chat (when active).
memory.forbidden: This area is only available for owner and colleague accounts.
memory.workspaceNew: Not created yet — save will create it
memory.ragsEmpty: No RAGs yet. Add one to use @code in prompts.

memory.ragJsonInvalid: Value must be valid JSON when type is JSON.
memory.codeHint: unique globally; use as @code in chat

webhooks.title: Connect Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: Owner only: issue and manage Bearer API keys per apex domain. Public Connect Webhook / Smart Router LLM traffic is invoked from the partner’s servers — not from this app.
webhooks.guideIntro: Give each partner one API key tied to their apex domain. They integrate from their own servers — not from this dashboard.
webhooks.guideBullet1: Keep the Bearer secret on the partner backend only. Do not ship it to browsers or mobile clients.
webhooks.guideBullet2: Send HTTP POST with Content-Type application/json. Body includes messages: an array of { role, content } with role limited to system, user, or assistant (no tools / skill-calling in this API).
webhooks.guideBullet3: Headers: Authorization: Bearer <key>; Origin or Referer must use a host allowed for that key (registered domain, or subdomains if you enabled them). Server-to-server calls should set Origin or Referer to the partner site URL.
webhooks.guideBullet4: Optional JSON fields: temperature, maxTokens, model (to pin a specific model instead of router default).
webhooks.guideBullet5: Success responses return model text, token usage, and routing metadata. For browser-side calls you must configure CORS on the API gateway; calling from the partner backend avoids that.
webhooks.guideExampleUrl: Example chat URL (prepend your configured API host):
webhooks.guideUrlNote: If your deployment mounts routes under another prefix (e.g. /api/v1), adjust the path to match your server.
webhooks.guideDocPushClarify: This is not a “callback webhook” where Mira pushes to the partner. The partner only sends HTTP POST to your Mira server. There are two public URLs with the same logic and the same body/response — only the path name differs so you can label integrations clearly.
webhooks.guideDocS1Intro: Same behavior for both paths: POST with JSON body, 200 JSON response (not wrapped in data).
webhooks.guideDocColPath: Path (after base URL of the Nest host)
webhooks.guideDocBaseHint: Paths include the global /api/v1 prefix, then connect-webhooks/v1/… as registered on the public controller.
webhooks.guideDocFullUrlsTitle: Full URLs (from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in this app):
webhooks.guideDocUrlPlaceholder: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to see full example URLs. Pattern:
webhooks.guideDocS2a: Owner creates an API key for the domain in this admin UI (JWT) and stores the mira_cw_… secret once (see CONNECT_WEBHOOKS.md admin section).
webhooks.guideDocS2b: The registered host must match Origin or Referer (required by default; disable only with CONNECT_WEBHOOK_REQUIRE_ORIGIN=false in trusted environments).
webhooks.guideDocS3origin: Usually required: URL whose host matches the registered domain. For server-to-server calls, set e.g. Origin: https://partner.com
webhooks.guideDocS4messages: Array of { role, content } with role in system, user, assistant (limits apply in code).
webhooks.guideDocS4temp: Optional sampling temperature for the provider.
webhooks.guideDocS4model: If set, forces a specific model and skips the router for model selection.
webhooks.guideDocS4noTools: Do not use a tool role or send tools; tool_calls from the model are rejected (400).
webhooks.guideDocS5alt: YOUR_HOST is your Nest base (same as NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL). YOUR_REGISTERED_DOMAIN must match the key’s registered domain.
webhooks.guideDocS6Note: Plain JSON (no Nest data wrapper). content: assistant text; usage: token counts; routing: Smart Router metadata (intent, tier, …).
webhooks.guideDocS7r400: Invalid body; model returned tool calls (rejected).
webhooks.guideDocS7r403: Key inactive or expired; missing Origin/Referer; host not allowed for the key.
webhooks.guideDocS7r429: Waited in rate-limit queue too long (default 3 minutes).
webhooks.ownerOnly: This page is only available to the owner account.
webhooks.empty: No keys yet. Add one for an apex domain (e.g. partner.com).
webhooks.created: Webhook key created. Store the secret safely — it will not be shown again.
webhooks.rotated: Key rotated. Save the new secret — it will not be shown again.
webhooks.domainHint: Apex domain only, no path. Hostname is normalized; must be unique.
webhooks.confirmRotate: Rotate the API key? The old key will stop working immediately after rotation.
webhooks.confirmDelete: Delete this API key? Usage history will be removed.
webhooks.secretWarn: Copy this Bearer token now. It cannot be retrieved later.
webhooks.usageScopeHint: Per domain. Based on successful webhook calls recorded by the server.
webhooks.filterHint: Leave both empty for the default (last 30 days). Set both to filter, or both empty and Apply to reset.
webhooks.noUsageEvents: No individual events in this range.
webhooks.usageRangeBothOrNone: Set both “from” and “to”, or clear both for the default (last 30 days).

mySetting.title: Personal credentials
mySetting.subtitle: Provider keys and endpoints stored for your account only. They override global defaults when set. Values from the server are masked.
mySetting.hint: Leave a field empty to keep the current saved value. Use Remove to clear your override (fall back to global config). Only changed fields are sent.
mySetting.oauthIntro: Connect via ChatGPT OAuth Codex (browser flow). Tokens are written to your user_config by POST /user-config/connect/chatgpt-oauth.
mySetting.oauthCodexHint: Same modes as global Settings (start → browser → finish). Tokens are stored only for your account (not global config).
mySetting.oauthRemovePending: Saved OAuth will be removed when you save.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Owner-only config management for providers and scheduler.
settings.forbidden: This area is only available for owner accounts.
settings.schedulerTimezoneHint: Cron uses this zone. Empty or invalid values fall back to UTC on the server.
settings.schedulerClockSavedOnlyHint: Uses the timezone already saved on the server. Save changes first if you edited the field above.
settings.schedulerTzFallbackWarning: Stored value differs from effective zone — invalid or empty IANA may fall back to UTC.
settings.openaiCodexOauthHint: Use this flow to connect ChatGPT/Codex OAuth directly from Settings.
settings.oauthCallbackPlaceholder: Paste callback URL or code from ChatGPT OAuth
settings.oauthStartHint: Opened OAuth URL. After consent, paste callback URL below and click Finish.
settings.oauthCallbackRequired: Please paste callback URL or code.
settings.oauthFinishHint: Finish request accepted, please check callback URL and try again.
settings.kieApiKeyHint: From kie.ai — enables image generation via the image_generate skill (gpt4o-image, seedream, nano-banana, …). Stored like other keys; masked when loaded from View config.
